Nvidia builds chips that do everything. Etched builds chips that do one thing, inference, and claims to do it far better. On Thursday the bet got a lot more money behind it. The startup raised $300 million in a Series C round that values it at $10.3 billion. Sequoia led […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Drone makers race to build navigation that survives warzone jamming https://thenextweb.com/news/drone-navigation-gps-jamming-quantum-farnborough Thu, 23 Jul 2026 16:35:54 +0000 TheNextWeb=4ee9d36dd00432470f1f693d6d3c8caa GPS spoofing and jamming have become standard countermeasures on the battlefield in Ukraine, separating drones from their operators and rendering some guided munitions useless. US-made Excalibur artillery shells fell out of favour after initial successes because their GPS guidance was jammable. Even fibre-optic control wires have been countered by entangling lines strung across fields. The […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Apple’s privacy policy reveals an AI shopping assistant is coming to the Apple Store app https://thenextweb.com/news/apple-store-app-ai-shopping-assistant-privacy-policy Thu, 23 Jul 2026 16:05:18 +0000 TheNextWeb=cf1759c4f2a8f2db557d60e88cebc7bf Apple is preparing to add an AI-powered shopping assistant to the Apple Store app, according to a privacy policy update spotted by MacRumors. The updated document describes a “Virtual Shopping Assistant” that collects account information, device identifiers, carrier information, chat data, and optionally location data to personalise responses. The feature is not live yet. Apple […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Cambridge built a planet-scale AI model, and skipped Nvidia to do it https://thenextweb.com/news/tessera-cambridge-earth-observation-ai-amd-vultr Thu, 23 Jul 2026 16:04:46 +0000 TheNextWeb=f40680fe78838acdc5e7b16e7593c6d0 The AI industry runs on Nvidia. A team at the University of Cambridge has just shown it does not have to. They have built a planetary-scale foundation model for the Earth, and every chip that trained and ran it came from AMD. The figure, disclosed separately from capital expenditure in a quarterly filing, represents purchases Google has already committed to under supply agreements and open purchase orders. Weeks later, a pulmonary embolism brought him, in his words, “to the […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Nvidia’s open-source simulator trains surgical robots in under two minutes https://thenextweb.com/news/nvidia-isaac-medical-physics-simulation-surgical-robots Thu, 23 Jul 2026 15:25:57 +0000 TheNextWeb=1b9d3448c87a77b70645d5888422c16f The hard part of medical robotics is not the robot. Nvidia thinks the answer is to let the robot rehearse inside a simulated body, millions of times over.
